Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check current square positions. The UP action changed 102 cells, which is more than 50 (just the squares). This suggests both squares moved, plus the bars changed.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current square positions
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'S':
        print(f"S: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
So now:
- Left square: rows 44-48, cols 19-23
- Right square: rows 49-53, cols 39-43
They're still at different vertical positions. The left square moved up from 49-53 to 44-48, and the right square moved up from 54-58 to 49-53.
So the squares are always offset by one chamber (5 rows). The left square can be at 39-43, 44-48, or 49-53. The right square can be at 39-43, 44-48, 49-53, or 54-58.
When the left square is at 49-53, the right square is at 54-58. When the left square is at 44-48, the right square is at 49-53. When the left square is at 39-43, the right square is at 44-48.
So the squares are NEVER at the same vertical position, except when... wait, let me check the initial state. Initially, both squares were at rows 49-53. But now I can't get them back to that configuration.
Actually, let me re-examine. Initially, the squares were at rows 49-53 (both). After pressing UP, the left square moved to 44-48 and the right square moved to... wait, initially the right square was at 49-53. After pressing UP, the right square should move to 44-48. But I observed that after pressing UP twice from the initial state, the left square was at 39-43 and the right square was at 44-48. This means after one UP, both squares were at 44-48.
But now, after pressing UP from the state where left is at 49-53 and right is at 54-58, the left square is at 44-48 and the right square is at 49-53. So the squares are offset.
This is confusing. Let me track the square positions more carefully.
Initial state: left at 49-53, right at 49-53 (both at same rows)
After UP: left at 44-48, right at 44-48 (both moved up)
After UP again: left at 39-43, right at 44-48 (left moved up, right stayed)
After DOWN: left at 44-48, right at 49-53 (left moved down, right moved down)
After DOWN again: left at 49-53, right at 54-58 (left moved down, right moved down)
After UP: left at 44-48, right at 49-53 (left moved up, right moved up)
So the squares can be at the same vertical position (49-53 or 44-48), but only in certain configurations. When the left square is at 39-43, the right square is at 44-48 (offset). When the left square is at 49-53, the right square can be at 49-53 (aligned) or 54-58 (offset).
Wait, this doesn't make sense. Let me re-examine.
Actually, I think the issue is that the corridor chambers are not symmetric. The left corridor has chambers at rows 39-43, 44-48, and 49-53. The right corridor has chambers at rows 39-43, 44-48, 49-53, and 54-58.
When both squares are at rows 49-53, they're aligned. When the left square moves up to 44-48, the right square also moves up to 44-48 (if it was at 49-53). But if the right square is at 54-58, moving up puts it at 49-53.
So the squares can be aligned (both at 49-53 or both at 44-48 or both at 39-43) or offset (left at 49-53, right at 54-58, etc.).
Now, I'm at left 44-48, right 49-53. Let me try pressing UP again to get left to 39-43 and right to 44-48. Then press UP again to get left to... 39-43 (can't go further) and right to 39-43. So both squares would be at 39-43!
Let me try this.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try to get both squares to rows 39-43
# Current: left at 44-48, right at 49-53
# UP: left at 39-43, right at 44-48
# UP: left stays at 39-43, right at 39-43
action(['UP', 'UP'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
